title: Basic SELECT Query with Multiple Clauses
description: Demonstrates the structure and order of SQL SELECT statement with WHERE, GROUP BY, HAVING, and ORDER BY
code:
SELECT department, COUNT(*) AS emp_count, AVG(salary) AS avg_salary
FROM employees
WHERE designation = 'Manager'
GROUP BY department
HAVING COUNT(*) > 5
ORDER BY avg_salary DESC;
explanation: This query selects department, counts employees, and calculates average salary for managers, filters departments with more than 5 managers, orders by average salary descending.

title: Creating View
description: Creating a view to simplify access to complex query results
code:
CREATE VIEW dept_salary_summary AS
SELECT d.department_name, e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.salary
FROM employees e
JOIN departments d ON e.department_id = d.department_id
WHERE e.salary > 50000
ORDER BY d.department_name, e.salary DESC;
explanation: Creates a virtual table showing high-earning employees (salary > 50000) with department names, simplifying repeated queries.

title: Stored Procedure with Parameters
description: Demonstrates creating a stored procedure with input and output parameters
explanation: Stored procedure accepts employee ID as input and returns name and salary as output parameters, demonstrating reusable database logic.

title: Trigger Example
description: Creating a trigger to automatically update a last_modified timestamp
code:
CREATE TABLE employee_audit (
 audit_id NUMBER PRIMARY KEY,
 emp_id NUMBER,
 action_type VARCHAR2(10),
 change_date DATE
);

CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER trg_employee_audit
AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE ON employees
FOR EACH ROW
BEGIN
 INSERT INTO employee_audit (audit_id, emp_id, action_type, change_date)
 VALUES (emp_audit_seq.NEXTVAL, :NEW.employee_id, 
 CASE 
 WHEN INSERTING THEN 'INSERT'
 WHEN UPDATING THEN 'UPDATE'
 END, SYSDATE);
END;
explanation: Trigger automatically creates an audit log entry whenever an employee record is inserted or updated, maintaining history of changes.

title: Embedded SQL in C Program
description: Demonstrates how SQL is embedded in C programming language
explanation: Shows SQL embedded within C code using SQL preprocessor directives (EXEC SQL), demonstrating host language integration with database.
